summary: Full E2E frontend testing of the client-side Gashapon capsule vending machine. All 12 requested features verified end-to-end: IDLE → INVOICE → PROCESSING → VERIFIED → DISPENSING → DROPPED → REVEALED, plus determinism/sessionStorage/reset and mobile responsive layout. 100% pass rate on functional flow.
critical_code_review_comments: Real blockchain polling (blockchain.js pollSweep) hits sochain.com and other explorers that either 400/430 or fail CORS. This produces 4 console errors on every INVOICE. Consider suppressing/catching those fetch errors silently or gating polling behind a feature flag to keep console clean (functional impact: none, DEMO button drives success).
Two elements share data-testid='simulate-payment-button' (one in INVOICE, one in PROCESSING). Only one is mounted at a time so tests still work, but ideally unique testids per state.

sessionStorage snapshot writes on every state change including empty logs — fine, but consider not persisting during PROCESSING to avoid restoring into an unrecoverable in-flight state (currently OK because PROCESSING falls back and DEMO still works).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Verified: LTC address ltc1q... from SECURE_ADDRESS_POOL; XMR address 4Bpq...; canvas is exactly 384x384 (32*12); category deterministic; download filename gasha-<cat>-<hash8>.png; play-again clears sessionStorage; mobile 390px stacks column with no horizontal scroll; desktop 1440px is row. Console errors from real explorer polling (sochain 400/430, CORS) are expected per problem statement — DEMO Simulate button is the intended path.
